blurb_en:
The U.S. Commerce Department cleared Anthropic’s Mythos 5 for access by over 100 vetted companies and agencies on Friday — the same day OpenAI launched its new GPT-5.6 model family under near-identical government-controlled conditions.
• GPT-5.6 comes in three: Sol (flagship, $30/M output tokens), Terra (balanced, $15/M), and Luna (fast, $6/M) — Sol leads on long-horizon cybersecurity and biology benchmarks.
• Anthropic’s Mythos 5, pulled from public access June 12 after security researchers demonstrated its guardrails could be bypassed, was cleared by Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick for a controlled release to over 100 specific U.S. agencies and businesses.
• OpenAI said the restrictions “shouldn’t be the long-term default” — then complied with them anyway, with Altman telling staff the government was approving access customer by customer.
• Both models are explicitly optimized for cybersecurity — which explains both why the government cares and why it wants to control the rollout.
• The AI company that spent years promising to give AI to the world is now on a government-approved customer list.
• For any creative or imaging company tracking AI capabilities: the most powerful tools may stay behind this clearance wall for months — subscribing won’t be enough.
blurb_zh:
特朗普政府上周五化身AI准入委员会：商务部长卢特尼克批准Anthropic的Mythos 5向逾百家经认证的美国企业与机构开放，同日OpenAI也在几乎相同的政府管控条件下发布了GPT-5.6系列新模型。
• GPT-5.6分三款：Sol（旗舰，每百万输出 token $30）、Terra（均衡，$15）、Luna（快速，$6），Sol在长周期网络安全与生物学任务上据称领先GPT-5.5。
• Anthropic的Mythos 5因6月12日安全研究人员证明其护栏可被绕过而被迫下架，此次以受控方式重新对逾百家机构开放。
• OpenAI表示这种管控“不该成为长期惯例”，说这话的同时他们已经同意了——Altman向员工说明政府在“逐一客户审批”接入资格。
• 两款模型均明确针对网络安全场景优化——这既解释了政府为何在意，也解释了为何政府要亲自把关。
• 对影像与相机行业来说：最强 AI工具短期内可能都待在国家安全的许可证之后，光有订阅账号用不了。
verification_notes: Confirmed via TechCrunch (GPT-5.6 restricted rollout June 26), TechCrunch (Trump admin releases Mythos June 26), CNBC, Axios, The Decoder. Multiple credible outlets confirmed both developments on same day. Mythos 5 cleared by Commerce Secretary Lutnick confirmed across TechCrunch, CNBC, Washington Examiner.

blurb_en:
Anthropic told the U.S. Senate Banking Committee this week that Alibaba used roughly 25,000 fraudulent accounts to run 28.8 million exchanges with Claude between April 22 and June 5 — what Anthropic is calling the largest known model distillation attack against any AI company to date.
• Model distillation means prompting a target AI repeatedly, at scale, to harvest its reasoning patterns and use them to train a cheaper copy — it’s how you steal capabilities without paying for a decade of research.
• The capabilities targeted were Claude’s most commercially valuable: agentic reasoning, software engineering proficiency, and long-horizon task completion.
• The letter, dated June 10, was addressed to Senate Banking Committee chairs Tim Scott and Elizabeth Warren; Anthropic called the campaign “brazenly” and “illicit.”
• The Alibaba campaign at 28.8 million exchanges exceeds the combined total of all three previous Chinese lab disclosures from February — DeepSeek, Moonshot AI, and MiniMax together clocked roughly 16 million.
• Alibaba denies wrongdoing.
• If Chinese AI labs can close the capability gap through distillation rather than original research, the competitive timeline for AI-powered creative and imaging tools shrinks considerably — including for camera companies watching which AI gets baked into their competitors’ products.
blurb_zh:
Anthropic本周致信美国参议院銀行委员会，指控阿里巴巴使用2.5万个虚假账号，于今年4月22日至6月5日对Claude发2880万次交互，实施所谓“对抗性蒸馏攻击”——Anthropic称这是迄今已知规模最大的AI模型盗取行动。
• “模型蒸馏”：反复大规模向目标AI提问，提取其推理模式，再用来训练自家模型——相当于不花十年研发经费的逆向工程。
• 被针对的能力包括：智能代理推理、软件工程熟练度、长周期任务完成——正是Claude企业端最有商业价值的核心技能。
• 此次攻击规模超过Anthropic今年2月公开披露的DeepSeek、Moonshot AI和MiniMax三家中国AI公司合计的约1600万次交互。
• 阿里巴巴否认不当行为。
• 如果中国AI公司能靠蒸馏攻击追上顶尖西方模型，影像与相机行业的AI竞争格局也会随之加速——你的竞争对手产品里，可能很快就内置了蒸馏而来的Claude能力。
verification_notes: Confirmed via Bloomberg (June 24), CNBC (June 24), Tom's Hardware (detailed 25,000 accounts 28.8M exchanges April 22-June 5), TechTimes (Senate Banking Committee context), Yahoo Finance. All sources consistent on scale, dates, capabilities targeted, and Alibaba denial.

blurb_en:
Figma held Config 2026 in San Francisco last week and shipped three things at once: Code Layers (executable code embedded directly on the design canvas), Figma Motion (native animation and transition support), and an AI plugin builder that writes working Figma plugins from a plain text description.
• Code Layers let teams pull a live code repository into Figma, extract UI flows into design layers, and annotate them alongside the source — the goal is one source of truth between design and development.
• Figma Motion rolled out June 24; previously you’d design the interface in Figma and animate it somewhere else entirely.
• The AI plugin builder generates working plugins from a one-line natural language description — either the fastest way to customize your workflow or the fastest way to automate someone out of a job, depending on where you sit.
• Figma also acquired Weavy, a node-based workflow tool; Weavy generation inside Figma is coming later in 2026.
• For Insta360’s design and creative teams, Code Layers is worth a test: if your handoff still involves exporting frames and narrating component states to developers, that friction might now be avoidable.
blurb_zh:
Figma在上周旧金山的Config 2026大会上一次性发布三项新功能：Code Layers（直接在设计画布上嵌入并运行代码）、Figma Motion（原生动画与过渡效果支持），以及可以用自然语言生成Figma插件的AI插件构建器。
• Code Layers允许团队将代码库直接拉入Figma，从代码中提取UI流程到设计图层并同步标注——目标是设计稿与代码之间只保留一个唯一来源。
• Figma Motion于6月24日上线；此前设计师通常在Figma里做界面、再去别的工具里做动效。
• AI插件构建器可以用一句话生成可运行的Figma插件——对设计师来说是利器，对Figma插件开发者来说不那么友好。
• Figma还收购了节点工作流工具Weavy，Weavy流程生成将于2026年晚些时候集成至Figma。
• 对Insta360设计团队来说，Code Layers尤其値得优先试：如果你的设计交付流程还在靠导出截图和口头解释，这个工具可能直接省掉这一步。
verification_notes: Confirmed via TechCrunch (June 24), Figma help center Config 2026 release notes, ExplainX Config 2026 recap, Helm News (June 25 summary). Code Layers, Figma Motion June 24 rollout, AI plugin builder, and Weavy acquisition all confirmed across multiple sources.
